title: fraxinus japonica var. stenocarpa
intro: Fraxinus japonica var. stenocarpa, also known as Manchurian ash, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its small to medium-sized leaves and distinctive leaf shape. The bark and leaves of the Manchurian ash possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of Fraxinus japonica var. stenocarpa include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular health, making it a popular herbal remedy in Eastern medicine. Fraxinus japonica var. stenocarpa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and treat specific health conditions.
